title: Populus rockii
intro: Populus rockii, also known as Rock's poplar or Chinese poplar, is a deciduous tree species native to China. The bark and leaves of Populus rockii have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Populus rockii include salicin, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Populus rockii is commonly prepared into decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to treat respiratory and circulatory issues, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate pain.
taxonomy: Populus rockii is classified within the family Salicaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales. Populus rockii is a species of poplar tree native to China. The genus Populus is commonly known as the poplar or aspens. Populus rockii is a deciduous tree, which is part of the broad category of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
uses: Populus rockii has been traditionally used in folk medicine in China for various purposes, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bark and leaves of the tree are used in traditional medicine, with the bark being valued for its anti-inflammatory properties. Populus rockii extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which could contribute to its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Populus rockii and its potential applications in modern medicine.
